prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: runtime tactics unit placeholder sprite for the setup screen
Primary request: create a neutral placeholder unit silhouette to represent an empty deployment slot
Subject: a single anonymous humanoid battle silhouette with no faction identity, no readable face, no emblem, and no unique gear language
Style/medium: moody fantasy tactics sprite illustration, painterly pixel-art-inspired, matching the existing Renations runtime unit art
Composition/framing: single full-body subject, centered, standing in a grounded idle pose, transparent background, tight sprite bounds, rendered around 100px tall in game
Lighting/mood: solemn ember-lit battlefield mood with restrained contrast and readable large shapes
Color palette: dark charcoal, warm ash gray, muted brass edging, faint parchment highlights
Constraints: the sprite must read clearly as an intentionally generic placeholder rather than a playable character; use a simplified hooded or cloaked silhouette with broad readable shapes and reduced micro-detail for downscaling; no faction colors, no distinctive weapon, no shield, no staff, no firearm, no magic effects, no floor, no scenery, no text, no extra subjects, no oversized empty padding
Avoid: recognizable unit identity, detailed face, glowing eyes, ornate armor, heraldry, extra props, photorealism, anime gloss, cinematic background, transparent holes through the body
